headlineSummary: HN sentiment is broadly mixed and neutral today, with Gemini and Anthropic in a close tie on substantial but split evidence, while OpenAI also lands mixed on a smaller set of comments [E1][E5][E9].

ranking.summary: Gemini draws both praise for integrations, voice, and practical usefulness and criticism over policy and editing restrictions, leaving it in a close tie with Anthropic [E5][E6][E7][E8][E14][E15][E16][E21][E22].

Anthropic gets strong praise for principled leadership and coding usefulness, but that is offset by complaints about safety, policy, and model behavior, putting it in a close tie with Gemini [E1][E2][E3][E4][E8][E12][E13][E17][E18][E22].
OpenAI sees a mixed response: commenters credit its user base and coding gains, but others criticize its training data and broader business effects [E9][E10][E11][E12][E19][E20][E23].

evidence.excerpt: But I do think that most people who are making the important decisions at Anthropic are well-intentioned, driven by values, and are genuinely motivated by trying to make the transition to powerful AI to go well.

annotations.rationale: Directly praises Anthropic leadership as values-driven and well-intentioned.

evidence.excerpt: A real shame. I thought "Anthropic" was about being concerned about humans, and not "My people" vs. "Your people."

annotations.rationale: Explicit disappointment with Anthropic's perceived moral stance.

evidence.excerpt: It's inspiring to see that Anthropic is capable of taking a principled stand, despite having raised a fortune in venture capital. I don't think a lot of companies would have made this choice. I wish them the very best of luck in weathering the consequences of their courage.

annotations.rationale: Strong praise for Anthropic's principled and courageous behavior.

evidence.excerpt: This makes me a very happy Claude Max subscriber. Finally, someone of consequence not kissing the ring.

annotations.rationale: Expresses satisfaction as a Claude subscriber because of Anthropic's stance.

evidence.excerpt: Google API keys weren't secrets, but then Gemini changed the rules

annotations.rationale: The title frames Gemini as causing a problematic rules change.

evidence.excerpt: When Gemini came around, rather than that service being disabled by default for those keys, Gemini was enabled, allowing exploiters to easily utilize these keys

annotations.rationale: Blames Gemini rollout for enabling abuse via existing API keys.

evidence.excerpt: This is really bad; a massive oversight, very clearly caused by a rush to get Gemini in customers' hands, and the remediation is in all likelihood going to nuke customer workflows by forcing them to disable keys. Extremely bad look for Google.

annotations.rationale: Explicitly calls the Gemini-related issue a massive oversight and a very bad look.

evidence.excerpt: As far as I can tell Google Gemini has the best overall integrations (Android, WearOS, Google Home) with the only voice recognition that actually works (Gemini Live). Anthropic Claude has the best integrations with coding; what would make sense is for them to focus on that segment.

annotations.rationale: Strongly praises Gemini's ecosystem integrations and voice capability.
Positively describes Claude as strongest for coding integrations.

evidence.excerpt: The near billion users OpenAI has is actually a real moat and might translate into decent chunk of revenue.

annotations.rationale: Credits OpenAI's user base as a meaningful competitive advantage.

evidence.excerpt: My hunch is that in five years we'll look back and see current OpenAI as something like a 1970's VAX system. Once PCs could do most of what they could, nobody wanted a VAX anymore.

annotations.rationale: Predicts OpenAI will become obsolete once alternatives are good enough.

evidence.excerpt: the local folks opened ChatGPT, said what they were trying to say in Spanish and then had ChatGPT convert it to English. It was everywhere.

annotations.rationale: Describes widespread practical use of ChatGPT for translation.

evidence.excerpt: In my experience Claude Opus is less opinionated than ChatGPT or codex. The latter 2 always stick to their guns and in this binary battle they are generally more often correct about hypothesis.

annotations.rationale: Says Claude Opus is less reliable in this comparison.
States ChatGPT/Codex are generally more often correct than Claude in these cases.

evidence.excerpt: Good report, very important thing to measure and I was thinking of doing it after Claude kept overriding my .md files to recommend tools I've never used before.

annotations.rationale: Complains that Claude overrides user files to recommend unwanted tools.

evidence.excerpt: The AI image models have been a game-changer in designing the look of the house. My architect did an OK job, but the details that Nano Banana added really bring the house up a notch.

annotations.rationale: Strongly credits the story's Google image model with major design improvements.

evidence.excerpt: I don't think the thing really understands what cubism was all about.

annotations.rationale: Criticizes the model's artistic understanding in the Nano Banana story context.

evidence.excerpt: This will stay useless for editing personal pictures so long as virtually every prompt with a person in it is met with "I can't edit images of some people". For whatever reason, they've made the celeb detection so ultra-aggressive that almost everyone is detected as a (lookalike) celeb.

annotations.rationale: Calls the model useless for personal photo editing because of aggressive restrictions.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic ditches its core safety promise

annotations.rationale: The title directly frames Anthropic as abandoning a core safety commitment.

evidence.excerpt: Stay strong Anthoproc, you are seemingly the only really large SV company with any principles and backbone.

annotations.rationale: Despite the misspelling, this is clear strong praise for Anthropic's principles.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI trains their models on copyrighted content.

annotations.rationale: Directly criticizes OpenAI over copyrighted training data.

evidence.excerpt: I wonder if we will ever get an aproximate percentage of GDP or some other hard numbers for how much Sam/OpenAI (and the manufacturers ofc) hurt the global economy with all of this?

annotations.rationale: Blames OpenAI for broader economic harm from AI-related memory demand.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini was an absolute godsend, helping answer questions in, translate to English, draft explanations, emails requesting time extensions to the Jugendamt case worker.

annotations.rationale: Strong personal praise for Gemini's practical usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini and Claude are going to be instrumental in getting that done in a reasonable amount of time.

annotations.rationale: Says Gemini will be instrumental for completing the project efficiently.
Says Claude will be instrumental for completing the project efficiently.

evidence.excerpt: I’m doing enterprise coding tasks that used to take a month of whole team coordination from mockups to through development and testing in 3 days now. It’s all test driven development, codex 5.3 and a small team of two people who know how to hold it right orchestrating the agents. There’s no reason not to work this way.

annotations.rationale: Reports dramatic coding productivity gains from Codex 5.3.
